name: mcp-broker
description: Local MCP broker that exposes many configured upstream MCP servers through one client entry.
long_description: mcp-broker runs a local broker process and a compact stdio facade so MCP clients can connect to one local server instead of loading every upstream tool directly.

tools.name: broker_search_tools
tools.description: Search the profile-visible upstream MCP tool catalog without advertising every upstream tool to the client. Use this first when the task mentions a capability, service, keyword, or upstream prefix and you need candidate broker-qualified tool names before describing or calling one of them.

tools.name: broker_describe_tool
tools.description: Describe one profile-visible upstream MCP tool by broker-qualified name. Use this after broker_search_tools and before broker_call_tool so the client can inspect the exact description, input schema, upstream owner, transport, and mutating metadata instead of guessing arguments.

tools.name: broker_call_tool
tools.description: Call one profile-visible upstream MCP tool through the broker using its broker-qualified name and exact argument object. The broker's mutating-tool policy remains gated by the active profile's allowlist, so call broker_describe_tool first and pass only arguments accepted by the described upstream schema.

tools.name: broker_status
tools.description: Report broker and upstream status for the active profile without starting hidden or denied upstreams. Use this for health checks, support tickets, and client validation because it returns the profile name, broker socket path, aggregate status, upstream runtime state, auth state, transport, process id, restarts, and mutating exposure.

uvx_path.title: UVX command path
uvx_path.description: Command or absolute path for uvx. Use an absolute path if Claude Desktop cannot find uvx from its GUI environment.

runtime_root.title: Runtime directory
runtime_root.description: Directory for broker runtime state, logs, sockets, and generated private config.

socket_path.title: Socket path
socket_path.description: Unix socket path used by the broker client facade.

config_path.title: Config path
config_path.description: Private broker YAML config. The broker creates it from the public example when missing.

profile.title: Client profile
profile.description: Broker profile used to filter and expose upstream MCP tools.
